To auto-fetch latest month: hit the API, take data[FY=current].acf_fields.disclosure_files[0].document_link.url.

HOW THE PORTAL WORKS (reverse-engineered from main.f9c065d3f8b0ebc6.js + runtime chunk map):
1. The reports component ('ft-fund-reports') fetches its document list client-side. Media/news tabs call: GET https://www.franklintempletonindia.com/api/literature/v1/documents?channel=en-in&type=<TYPE>&cache=true (built in the bundle as `?channel=en-in&type=${tabType}&cache=true`). I could NOT enumerate the exact <TYPE> token for the reports/monthly-portfolio category (~40 guesses incl. fundReports, monthlyPortfolio, fund-portfolio, cust-report, institutional-reports all → 204 empty). The page also lazy-loads the 'xlsx' parser chunk (node_modules_xlsx_xlsx_mjs) — confirming it serves real .xlsx workbooks.
2. The brxm page-model API GET https://www.franklintempletonindia.com/resourceapi/investor/reports returns the 'Page Not Found' content variant FROM THIS DATACENTER IP (the ft-fund-reports component is absent). This is geo/edge gating (the edge geolocates the egress IP; client geo headers like X-Forwarded-For / CF-IPCountry:IN are ignored). So the live Monthly-Portfolio asset list cannot be read from this environment.

FILE HOSTING — VERIFIED REACHABLE + DOWNLOADABLE FROM HERE: all Franklin India documents live on the Widen DAM CDN. The /view/pdf/<id>/<file>.xlsx URL returns an HTML viewer; the TRUE direct-download form is /content/<assetId>/original/<filename>.xlsx?download=true&u=peewry . I downloaded a real Franklin India .xlsx this way and magic-byte-verified it: HTTP 200, content-type application/vnd.openxmlformats-...spreadsheetml.sheet, 672310 bytes, bytes b'PK\\x03\\x04' (redirects to cf-store.widencdn.net). Example proven URL: https://franklintempletonprod.widen.net/content/cv6j32ncer/original/Disclosure-of-Votes-Exercised-FY-2024-2025-for-AuditTab.xlsx?download=true&u=peewry  (this is the 'Disclosure of Votes' file, NOT the monthly portfolio — used only to prove the CDN+download mechanism works from here).

WHY found=false: I verified the DOWNLOAD MECHANISM (Widen /content/<id>/original/<file>.xlsx?download=true → PK-verified) but NOT the specific MONTHLY-PORTFOLIO asset, because its opaque per-month assetId lives in the geo-gated reports page model that 404s from this IP. There is NO date-derivable URL — Widen assetIds are random hashes per upload, so future months cannot be auto-generated from a template; the assetId MUST be read fresh each month from the portal's literature-API JSON.

LOCAL BROWSER HARNESS PLAYBOOK (India egress): (a) open https://www.franklintempletonindia.com/investor/reports?firstFilter-12 in a headless browser; (b) navigate to the Reports tab whose secondFilter is the Monthly Portfolio / portfolio-disclosure category; (c) capture the XHR to /api/literature/v1/documents?channel=en-in&type=<TYPE>&cache=true and read each doc's links.site.href (a widen.net URL); (d) for the latest month, rewrite /view/pdf/<id>/<file> -> /content/<id>/original/<file>.xlsx?download=true&u=peewry and GET it (expect PK magic). Alternatively scrape the rendered table rows directly for the widen hrefs. The 'u=peewry' token is the Widen account/CDN qualifier seen on every Franklin India asset.

FALLBACK SOURCE (recommended for a reliable pipeline): AMFI's standardized monthly portfolio disclosure index or a mirror aggregator (e.g. advisorkhoj form-download-centre) exposes Franklin monthly portfolios without the geo gate — worth wiring as the resilient path since the AMC's own CDN asset IDs are non-deterministic.

Step 1 (resolver, this gives the file for any month): GET https://www.utimf.com/api/get-consolidate-portfolio-disclosure?year={YYYY}&month={Month} with a desktop User-Agent and header Accept: application/json; {YYYY}=4-digit year, {Month}=full English month name e.g. "May". It returns JSON {"rows":[{"url":"<direct cloudfront .zip>", "doc":"<same>", "name":..., "type":"Zip", "month":..., "year":...}]}. Step 2: take rows[0].url verbatim (already includes the required ?VersionId= token) and GET it from d3ce1o48hc5oli.cloudfront.net -> that is the actual workbook ZIP. "Latest month" = iterate months newest-first; the current/just-ended month returns {"rows":[]} until UTI publishes (~15-25 days after month-end), so the latest available is the most recent month whose rows[] is non-empty (presently 2026/May). The CloudFront file path itself is INCONSISTENT month-to-month (observed: s3fs-public/{pub-YYYY-MM}/[fw_]uti_mf_scheme_portfolios_{DD.MM.YYYY}[_0|_1|_2].zip AND a totally different static/generic-zip/{Mon-YY}/FW_%20UTI_MF_Scheme_portfolios-{DD.MM.YYYY}.zip), so it must NOT be guessed — always resolve via the API.

zerodha.url_pattern: https://assets.zerodhafundhouse.com/statutory-reports/portfolio-disclosures/{CODE}{SEP}Monthly Portfolio {Month} {YYYY}.xlsx  — where {Month} is the full English month name (e.g. May), {YYYY} is the 4-digit year, and {CODE}{SEP} is the per-scheme prefix. IMPORTANT: the separator is INCONSISTENT per scheme and must be looked up, not assumed. For May 2026 the verified prefixes are: 'ZBSEN - ', 'ZE100 - ', 'ZE150- ', 'ZELSS - ', 'ZEN50- ', 'ZESML- ', 'ZGFOF - ', 'ZGOLD - ', 'ZLIQD - ', 'ZLTGC - ', 'ZMIDS - ', 'ZMLTI - ', 'ZN250 - ', 'ZNFTY - ', 'ZNH73 - ', 'ZNSDG - ', 'ZOVER - ', 'ZSFOF - ', 'ZSLVR - ' (note ZE100/ZE150/ZEN50/ZESML use no leading space before the hyphen). Spaces must be URL-encoded as %20 when requesting. To auto-build next month: keep each scheme's separator as listed and substitute the new {Month} {YYYY}. There is one file per scheme (no single consolidated file in recent months).
zerodha.date_basis: The month and year appear as plain English words inside the filename: "Monthly Portfolio May 2026" (full month name + 4-digit year). There is NO financial-year folder and NO day-of-month; the file lives in a single flat folder /statutory-reports/portfolio-disclosures/. Each MUTUAL-FUND SCHEME has its OWN file, prefixed by a 5-char scheme code (e.g. ZNFTY = Nifty 50, ZN250 = Nifty Next 250, ZGOLD = Gold ETF/FOF, ZLIQD = Liquid). Latest available month = MAY 2026.
